title: NIS2 Entity Classification Validation (Essential vs Important)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.2(1) is assigned contributing assurance because this UC provides concrete Splunk evidence for the observable control condition, while policy approval, legal interpretation, and official filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.2(1) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.31: NIS2 Entity Classification Validation (Essential vs Important).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.31' running on nis2_entity_classification.csv, CMDB export (index=cmdb or lookup), ServiceNow company records, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `lookup=nis2_entity_classification.csv` columns `legal_entity_id`, `entity_tier`, `registered_name` remain the legal baseline. `index=cmdb` `sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_server` (Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow, Splunkbase 1928) carries `nis2_entity_tag`, `company`, `sys_id`, `os`, `name`. Cross-check geography and sector using `lookup=nis2_entity_scope.csv` (`country_code`, `in_scope`, `entity_tier_expected`). Governance policy attestations may mirror `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` with `policy_number` ties.
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) optional for asset overlays
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
| join legal_entity_id type=left [
    search index=cmdb sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_server earliest=-1d
    | stats values(nis2_entity_tag) as cmdb_tags by legal_entity_id
]
| eval mismatch=if(isnull(cmdb_tags),1,if(NOT match(mvjoin(cmdb_tags,"|"), entity_tier),1,0))
| where mismatch=1
| table legal_entity_id, entity_tier, cmdb_tags, mismatch
description: Compares the legal NIS2 tier recorded in the classification lookup against CMDB tags synced from ServiceNow so mislabelled legal entities and the servers attached to them surface before reporting deadlines slip.
value: Prevents the wrong regulatory playbook—essential versus important—from silently propagating through automation by catching CMDB drift the week it happens, not when a supervisor asks for evidence months later.
implementation: (1) Legal publishes authoritative tiers into `nis2_entity_classification.csv`; block edits without CAB ticket. (2) ServiceNow CMDB business rules write `nis2_entity_tag` nightly. (3) Save `UC-22.2.31` daily. (4) Auto-create ServiceNow tasks for mismatches. (5) Map `nis2_entity_scope.csv` during cross-border reviews.
visualization: Table of mismatched entities, single value count needing review, optional Sankey from entity to CI counts.
knownFalsePositives:
• **CMDB lag after legal reclassification** — Distinguish: court filing updated but SNOW import still queued **24 hours**. Suppress: time-bound `nis2_classification_transition_register.csv` with `legal_effective_date`.
• **Multi-tier holdings in one legal entity** — Distinguish: `entity_tier` aggregate while CMDB tags list subsidiaries. Suppress: enrich with `mvappend` from lookup `nis2_subsidiary_map.csv` before `match()`.
• **Case-sensitive tags** — Distinguish: `Essential` vs `essential`. Suppress: `| eval entity_tier=lower(entity_tier)` pipeline macro.
• **Test CIs in production CI classes** — Distinguish: `host.class=test` still inherits stale tag. Suppress: `where NOT match(name,"^tst")` filter on CMDB subsearch.
• **Assetless legal shell** — Distinguish: no cmdb rows yet for brand-new SPV. Suppress: allow `mismatch=1` but route to legal queue not ops via lookup `nis2_zero_asset_entities.csv`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) on a Heavy Forwarder hitting `https://<instance>.service-now.com/api/now/table/cmdb_ci_server` every **300 seconds** with OAuth.
• Index `cmdb` sized for full enterprise CI counts; retention **400 days** to survive annual audits.
• CSV `nis2_entity_classification.csv` in Git with Splunk Deployment Server distribution.
• Secondary lookup `nis2_entity_scope.csv` capturing geography constraints; optional join in extended panel.
• RBAC limiting `cmdb` to architects and compliance analysts.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map ServiceNow fields `u_legal_entity` → `legal_entity_id`, `u_nis2_tag` → `nis2_entity_tag`. For multi-datacenter clones, add `splunk_server` partition to avoid duplicate CIs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Implement SPL, run daily, store results `append=t` to summary index optional. Alert when `mismatch=1` count >0.

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
| join legal_entity_id type=left [
    search index=cmdb sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_server earliest=-1d
    | stats values(nis2_entity_tag) as cmdb_tags by legal_entity_id
]
```

Understanding this SPL: `mvjoin` collapses tags before regex `match()` compares textual tier tokens—adjust pattern if tags encode JSON.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick a known aligned entity, ensure no row returned. Force a mismatch in lab CMDB row and confirm detection within one search run.

Step 4 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Create ServiceNow integration: alert action posts to `incident` table with `u_correlation_id`. If join fails, inspect `legal_entity_id` nulls on CIs—often missing corporate linkage field.

Step 5 — Governance
Legal counsel reviews CSV quarterly. Sign exports with `evidenceSigning`. During M&A freeze windows, pause alert via `nis2_exception_register.csv`.
grandmaExplanation: We double-check that the big organisation chart labels—who counts as "extra supervised" versus "regular supervised"—match what the inventory system thinks, so nobody follows the wrong rulebook by accident.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel/competent-authority review.
evidence: `UC-22.2.31` mismatch report with `legal_entity_id`, snapshot of CMDB tags, ServiceNow task numbers, and signed PDF in `audit_evidence` referencing Art.2(1) classification checks.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Set `legal_entity_id=LE-TEST` to `entity_tier=essential` in the lookup while CMDB events carry `nis2_entity_tag=important` for the same ID; run the search and confirm a row with `mismatch=1`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Align CMDB `nis2_entity_tag` text to include essential per the lookup (or remove the CI test data) and verify the next run produces no row for that entity.
